title: Improve testing coverage for `AdvectiveForcing` (and other forcings too)

body:
This is only a beginning, and I'll need help from others, there are literally hundreds of issues to address.  For example https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/81dc6c5db02ba111ffc750999e3ca83402dfebe3/src/Operators/spacings_and_areas_and_volumes.jl#L390-L436 defines some hundreds of methods which expect non-existing metrics to be there, while also exporting them:
```julia-repl
julia> using Oceananigans

julia> grid = RectilinearGrid(size=(4, 4, 4), extent=(1, 1, 1));

julia> Oceananigans.Operators.Az(1, 1, 1, grid, Nothing(), Center(), Face())
ERROR: UndefVarError: `Azᵃᶜᶠ` not defined in `Oceananigans.Operators`
```
`Oceananigans.Operators.Azᵃᶜᶠ` is even exported, same for all the other metrics involving `Nothing`.  Do I understand correctly `Nothing` should just be removed from the lists?

See https://juliatesting.github.io/Aqua.jl/stable/ for how to run the tests locally, for example I started with
```julia-repl
julia> using Aqua, Oceananigans

julia> Aqua.test_all(Oceananigans; ambiguities=false)
```
I skipped the ambiguities at the beginning, because there are 358 of them (yes, you read that right)

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

title: Run Aqua tests

body:
This is needed to use arrays for the closure coefficient on GPU, or functions like the example illustrates (the doctest example does build, but cannot be time-stepped).

Should probably test this.
